attributes.action: Ban driving insurance companies from charging more because of a drivers age
attributes.additional_details: Age-based pricing in car insurance is unfair and discriminatory. Many young and older drivers have clean records and drive safely, yet are charged more purely because of their age. Premiums should reflect individual behaviour and risk, not broad assumptions that penalise responsible drivers and limit access to work, education, and independence.
attributes.background: The Government should ban car insurance companies from charging higher premiums based solely on a driver’s age. Insurance prices should be based on individual risk factors such as driving record and claims history, not age-based assumptions.

rejection.details:
We already a have a petition open on this subject which you may wish to sign:

Ban higher car insurance premiums for young drivers
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/742967

You may also wish to sign this petition, which is on a related subject:

Require car insurance companies to insure the car, not the driver
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/740323
